name: Jacory Croskey-Merritt
headline: Explosive early-down runner crowded by a deeper Washington backfield
body:
Croskey-Merritt's rookie tape was genuinely explosive: 805 yards on 175 carries (4.6 per attempt) with eight scores, and the chunk plays jump off the page with 21 runs of 10-plus, three of 20-plus, and a 72-yard touchdown. He forced 13 broken tackles and ran behind a clean blocking picture, generating 2.53 yards before contact per attempt. The warning signs are real too: a -0.024 EPA per play, 17 carries for negative yardage, 17 stuffs at or behind the line, and three fumbles point to boom-or-bust volatility rather than steady efficiency.

The bigger concern for 2026 is opportunity. He opens camp atop the depth chart but enters a crowded room after Washington added Rachaad White, Jerome Ford, and rookie Kaytron Allen. With only nine catches and a 3% target share last year, he offers little passing-down value, and White or Allen could siphon goal-line work. Treat him as a Round 8 upside dart, not a guaranteed starter.
